perturbation.original_text: 17. Waiver of Trial by Jury. EACH PARTY HEREBY KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES THE RIGHT EITHER OF THEM MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT OF ANY LITIGATION BASED HEREON, OR ARISING OUT OF, UNDER OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT. THIS PROVISION IS A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR THE PARTIES ENTERING INTO THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Publicity. Counterparty shall not make any public disclosures regarding the Company , or the Transactions , including, without limitation, any advertisements, publications or documents, without the prior written approval of the Company . 17. Waiver of Trial by Jury. EACH PARTY HEREBY KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES THE RIGHT EITHER OF THEM MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT OF ANY LITIGATION BASED HEREON, OR ARISING OUT OF, UNDER OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT. THIS PROVISION IS A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR THE PARTIES ENTERING INTO THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jury waiver to be after the publicity clause weakens its prominence. Jury waivers should be conspicuous and stand out.
perturbation.law_explanation: By burying the jury waiver clause after a less important clause, it reduces the likelihood that it's knowingly and voluntarily agreed to, potentially undermining the right to a jury trial guaranteed by the Seventh Amendment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The perturbation type is 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction', defined as a required legal disclosure placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The modified text moves the jury waiver clause after the publicity clause, potentially reducing its prominence and the likelihood that it's knowingly and voluntarily agreed to. The Law Explanation states this undermines the right to a jury trial guaranteed by the Seventh Amendment. Scraped Law Snippet 2 discusses the Seventh Amendment, stating it preserves the right to a jury trial, which can be waived by parties. Moving the clause buries it within the contract, creating a structural flaw that contradicts the requirement that such waivers be knowing and voluntary.

perturbation.original_text: 19. No Warranties. Each Party acknowledges that the Disclosing Party provides the Confidential Information on an “as is” basis and without warranty of any kind. THE DISCLOSING PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES PERTAINING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Publicity. Counterparty shall not make any public disclosures regarding the Company , or the Transactions , including, without limitation, any advertisements, publications or documents, without the prior written approval of the Company . 19. No Warranties. Each Party acknowledges that the Disclosing Party provides the Confidential Information on an “as is” basis and without warranty of any kind. THE DISCLOSING PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES PERTAINING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the no warranties clause to be after the publicity clause weakens its prominence. No warranty disclaimers are crucial protections for the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-316 - Exclusion or Modification of Warranties
perturbation.law_explanation: By positioning the 'No Warranties' clause after the 'Publicity' clause, it reduces the prominence and potentially the enforceability of the warranty disclaimer, potentially conflicting with UCC § 2-316, which requires such disclaimers to be conspicuous. A less prominent placement makes it easier to argue that the disclaimer was not effectively communicated or understood, thereby undermining its validity.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. (1) Words or conduct relevant to the creation of an express warranty and words or conduct tending to negate or limit warranty shall be construed wherever reasonable as consistent with each other; but subject to the provisions of this Article on parol or extrinsic evidence (Section 2-202 ) negation or limitation is inoperative to the extent that such construction is unreasonable. (2)Subject to subsection (3), to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability or any part of it the language must mention merchantability and in case of a writing must be conspicuous, and to exclude or modify any implied warranty of fitness the exclusion must be by a writing and conspicuous. Language to exclude all implied warranties of fitness is sufficient if it states, for example, that "There are no warranties which extend beyond the description on the face hereof." (3)Notwithstanding subsection (2) (a) unless the circumstances indicate otherwise, all implied warranties are excluded by expressions like "as is", "with all faults" or other language which in common understanding calls the buyer's attention to the exclusion of warranties and makes plain that there is no implied warranty; and (b) when the buyer before entering into the contract has examined the goods or the sample or model as fully as he desired or has refused to examine the goods there is no implied warranty with regard to defects which an examination ought in the circumstances to have revealed to him; and (c) an implied warranty can also be excluded or modified by course of dealing or course of performance or usage of trade. (4) Remedies for breach of warranty can be limited in accordance with the provisions of this Article on liquidation or limitation of damages and on contractual modification of remed
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by potentially diminishing the prominence of the 'No Warranties' clause, moving it after the 'Publicity' clause. This contradicts UCC § 2-316, as explained in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, particularly subsection (2), states that to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability, the language must be conspicuous. By positioning the clause in a less prominent location, it becomes easier to argue that the disclaimer was not effectively communicated, thereby undermining its validity and conflicting with the requirement of conspicuousness.
